description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly light gray coloration and subtle hints of off-white and pale blue. Its surface is rough and uneven, featuring natural bumps, shallow depressions, and jagged edges typical of quarried or naturally weathered rocks. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications, suggesting it is composed primarily of a single mineral type, likely granite or limestone. The texture is matte and non-reflective, with a granular appearance that indicates a coarse grain structure. There are no visible veins, carvings, or significant color variations, giving it a uniform and natural look. This stone would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of landscaping features, serving as a natural obstacle or decorative element.
description: A light gray, irregularly shaped stone with a rough, granular surface and subtle color variations. Its dense, solid form and natural texture make it suitable for use as an urban landscaping or barrier element.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and flat with a slightly domed top and uneven edges, showing its largest surface area.
description_view_1: This view highlights a more angular, upright side with a pronounced corner and visible rough texture.
description_view_2: The stone looks flatter and more rounded from this angle, with gentle curves and fewer sharp edges.
description_view_3: A side view showing a triangular profile, with a sloped surface and a prominent ridge along the top.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loosely embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling with no distinct veining or artificial markings; the surface is visually uniform with natural granular texture.
attribute_weathering: Rough and slightly worn, with minor surface pitting and no significant lichen or moss, indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones in landscaping; may contrast with concrete or metal urban features but integrates well with greenery and earth.
